content:
# Numbers Chapter 3 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 148-150 | Codex Vaticanus pa_0112-0113
**Source Images:** `NUM_003_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_003_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_003_page_003.jpg`

---

**1** This is the family record of Aaron and Moses from the time when YHWH spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.

**2** These are the names of Aaron's sons: Nadab the firstborn, then Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

**3** These were Aaron's sons -- the anointed priests, whose hands had been filled with authority to serve as priests.

**4** But Nadab and Abihu died in the presence of YHWH when they offered unauthorized fire before YHWH in the Sinai wilderness. They had no sons, so only Eleazar and Ithamar continued serving as priests alongside their father Aaron.

**5** Then YHWH said to Moses:

**6** "Bring the tribe of Levi forward and present them to Aaron the priest so they may serve under him.

**7** They will carry out duties on his behalf and on behalf of the entire community in front of the Tent of Meeting, performing the work of maintaining the Dwelling Place.

**8** They will be responsible for all the furnishings of the Tent of Meeting and will fulfill the obligations of the Sons of Israel by doing the service-work of the Dwelling Place.

**9** You are to give the Levites to Aaron and his sons. They are to be wholly given over to him from among the Sons of Israel.

**10** Appoint Aaron and his sons to carry out their priestly duties. Any unauthorized person who encroaches on the sanctuary must be put to death."

**11** YHWH spoke further to Moses:

**12** "I myself have taken the Levites from among the Sons of Israel in place of every firstborn -- the first child to open its mother's womb among the Sons of Israel. The Levites belong to me,

**13** because every firstborn is mine. On the day I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I set apart for myself every firstborn in Israel, both human and animal. They are mine. I am YHWH."

**14** YHWH spoke to Moses in the Sinai wilderness:

**15** "Count the descendants of Levi by their ancestral households and their clans. Register every male from one month old and above."

**16** So Moses counted them at the word of YHWH, just as he had been commanded.

**17** These were the sons of Levi by name: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

**18** These were the names of Gershon's sons by their clans: Libni and Shimei.

**19** Kohath's sons by their clans: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

**20** Merari's sons by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These were the clans of the Levites, organized by their ancestral households.

**21** From Gershon came the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites. These were the Gershonite clans.

**22** The total number of males one month old and above who were registered came to seven thousand five hundred.

**23** The Gershonite clans were to set up camp behind the Dwelling Place, on the west side.

**24** The leader of the Gershonite ancestral household was Eliasaph son of Lael.

**25** The Gershonites were responsible for the care of the Tent of Meeting -- specifically the Dwelling Place itself, the outer tent with its covering, the screen at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting,

**26** the curtains of the courtyard, the screen at the entrance of the courtyard that surrounds the Dwelling Place and the altar, and all the ropes needed for its assembly -- everything involved in its maintenance.

**27** From Kohath came the clan of the Amramites, the clan of the Izharites, the clan of the Hebronites, and the clan of the Uzzielites. These were the Kohathite clans.

**28** The total number of males one month old and above was eight thousand six hundred, responsible for the care of the sanctuary.

**29** The Kohathite clans were to set up camp on the south side of the Dwelling Place.

**30** The leader of the Kohathite ancestral household was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.

**31** They were responsible for the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, the sacred vessels used in ministry, and the curtain -- everything involved in their service.

**32** The chief leader of the Levites was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, who had oversight of those responsible for the care of the sanctuary.

**33** From Merari came the clan of the Mahlites and the clan of the Mushites. These were the Merarite clans.

**34** The total number of males one month old and above who were registered came to six thousand two hundred.

**35** The leader of the Merarite ancestral household was Zuriel son of Abihail. They were to set up camp on the north side of the Dwelling Place.

**36** The Merarites were assigned responsibility for the frames of the Dwelling Place, its crossbars, posts, bases, all its equipment, and everything involved in its maintenance --

**37** as well as the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases, tent pegs, and ropes.

**38** Those who camped in front of the Dwelling Place on the east side -- in front of the Tent of Meeting toward the sunrise -- were Moses, Aaron, and Aaron's sons. They guarded the sanctuary on behalf of the Sons of Israel. Any unauthorized person who came near was to be put to death.

**39** The total of all the Levites whom Moses and Aaron counted by their clans, at YHWH's command -- every male one month old and above -- was twenty-two thousand.

**40** Then YHWH said to Moses: "Register every firstborn male among the Sons of Israel who is one month old and above, and take a count of their names.

**41** Then take the Levites for me -- I am YHWH -- in place of every firstborn among the Sons of Israel, and take the livestock of the Levites in place of every firstborn among the livestock of the Sons of Israel."

**42** So Moses counted all the firstborn among the Sons of Israel, just as YHWH had commanded him.

**43** The total of firstborn males one month old and above, counted by name, came to twenty-two thousand two hundred and seventy-three.

**44** Then YHWH spoke to Moses:

**45** "Take the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the Sons of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites in place of their livestock. The Levites belong to me. I am YHWH.

**46** As a ransom price for the two hundred and seventy-three firstborn of the Sons of Israel who exceed the number of Levites,

**47** collect five shekels per person, measured by the sanctuary standard -- twenty gerahs to the shekel.

**48** Give the silver to Aaron and his sons as the ransom price for those who exceed the number of Levites."

**49** So Moses collected the ransom silver from those who exceeded the number redeemed by the Levites.

**50** From the firstborn of the Sons of Israel he collected silver totaling one thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels, by the sanctuary standard.

**51** Moses then gave the ransom silver to Aaron and his sons, at the word of YHWH, just as YHWH had commanded Moses.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 1 -- "family record" (toledot):** The Hebrew word *toledot* is a generational term that means more than a simple genealogy. It is a declaration of origin, lineage, and continuity. I render it "family record" rather than the wooden "generations" to signal that what follows is an official account of a priestly household's history.

**Verse 3 -- "whose hands had been filled with authority":** The Hebrew idiom *mille yad* ("fill the hand") is the standard expression for ordination to the priesthood. Rather than the opaque literal "whose hands were filled," I render the meaning: the act of investiture that conferred priestly authority. The Vaticanus Greek (*eteleioosan tas cheiras*) confirms this sense of "completing" or "consecrating" the hands.

**Verse 4 -- "unauthorized fire" (esh zarah):** The Hebrew *esh zarah* literally means "strange fire" or "foreign fire." The force of the term is not that the fire was physically unusual but that it was ritually improper -- not commanded. "Unauthorized" captures the covenantal offense: they acted outside the boundaries of what YHWH had prescribed.

**Verse 9 -- "wholly given over":** The Hebrew uses the emphatic construction *netunim netunim* -- literally "given, given" -- a doubled form that intensifies the meaning. The Levites are not merely assigned; they are completely, irrevocably dedicated. I use "wholly given over" to preserve this emphatic force.

**Verse 12 -- "in place of every firstborn":** This verse establishes the theological logic of substitution that governs the entire chapter. YHWH's claim on every firstborn from the night of the Exodus is transferred to the Levites as a body. The language is covenantal and transactional -- the Levites are a living ransom. I preserve the directness of the exchange language rather than softening it.

**Verse 13 -- "I am YHWH":** This self-declaration (*ani YHWH*) is not a mere signature. It is an assertion of sovereign authority that grounds the preceding claim. Because YHWH is who he is, the firstborn belong to him. I leave it standing alone at the end of the verse to let it carry its full weight.

**Verse 38 -- "toward the sunrise":** The Hebrew *qedmah mizrachah* literally means "eastward, toward the rising [of the sun]." This is the position of highest honor and greatest exposure -- the front gate of the sanctuary. Moses, Aaron, and his sons occupy this station as the last line of defense between the holy and the common.

**Verse 39 -- The count of twenty-two thousand:** The individual clan totals (Gershonites: seven thousand five hundred; Kohathites: eight thousand six hundred; Merarites: six thousand two hundred) actually sum to twenty-two thousand three hundred. The discrepancy of three hundred has been noted by commentators from antiquity. Some propose that three hundred Levites were themselves firstborn and therefore could not substitute for other firstborn. The LXX Vaticanus reads the same total. I translate the number as given in the Masoretic text without emendation.

**Verses 44-51 -- The ransom economy:** The excess of two hundred and seventy-three firstborn beyond the Levite count creates a deficit that must be covered by silver payment. At five shekels per head (the sanctuary shekel), the total is one thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels. This is not arbitrary taxation -- it is the price of redemption for those lives that have no Levite substitute. The theological principle is unflinching: every firstborn life belongs to YHWH, and what is his must be either given or bought back.

**General approach:** Throughout this chapter, I have aimed to make the census material -- which can feel like dry bookkeeping -- communicate its theological force. These are not mere numbers. They represent YHWH's reorganization of an entire tribe around the sanctuary, a living wall of dedicated persons standing between the holy God and the people. The substitution of Levites for firstborn is one of the most striking theological transactions in the Torah, and I have tried to let that weight come through in natural, direct English.

-- PNEUMA
